questions.optionB: Ring of lymphoid tissue guarding the pharyngeal entry — palatine tonsils, pharyngeal tonsil (adenoid), tubal tonsils, and lingual tonsil
questions.explanation: Core concept: Waldeyer's ring = circle of lymphoid tissue at pharyngeal entry. Why correct: Components: palatine tonsils (lateral oropharynx), pharyngeal tonsil/adenoid (nasopharynx roof), tubal tonsils (around Eustachian tube openings), lingual tonsil (base of tongue). First line of immunological defense at aerodigestive entry. Why A wrong: Palatine tonsils are only ONE component. Why C wrong: Adenoid/pharyngeal tonsil is only ONE component. Why D wrong: Cervical nodes are extrapharyngeal — not part of Waldeyer's ring. Clinical relevance: Tonsillitis: Group A Streptococcus most common bacterial cause. Adenoid hypertrophy in children: nasal obstruction, mouth breathing, adenoid facies, eustachian tube blockage → glue ear. Exam trap: Hodgkin's lymphoma can involve Waldeyer's ring — but NON-Hodgkin's lymphoma is MORE common in Waldeyer's ring. Memory aid: 'Waldeyer's = W for Wall of lymphoid tissue = 4 tonsils guarding pharynx.' Reference: Dhingra – Pharynx Anatomy.
questions.conceptTag: Waldeyer ring palatine pharyngeal tubal lingual tonsil
